title: block-pi-context-glue.sh quote-strip is line-scoped -- a quoted span crossing a newline is not neutralized, so a multi-line quoted CLI mention false-positives the recognizer and glue heuristics
description: The quote-strip at .claude/hooks/block-pi-context-glue.sh line 32 uses perl -pe, which substitutes line by line, so a single- or double-quoted span that opens on one physical line and closes on a later line is never collapsed to its placeholder -- its interior leaks into the skeleton the recognizer (line 35) and the four glue heuristics (lines 43/48/59/64) consume. Confirmed live twice this session: a git commit whose multi-line -m message mentioned the CLI was blocked with the exit-capture message though it contains no pi-context op -- the message body's literal CLI-name mention matches the recognizer and the build pipeline's exit-capture echo matches the glue branch. Reduced repro: a 2-line double-quoted commit message mentioning the CLI blocks; the identical text on ONE line passes -- the sole delta is the newline. Class: the quote-strip neutralizes only single-line quoted spans; any quoted span crossing a newline leaks its interior metacharacters and CLI-name mentions. This is the residual multi-line facet of the closed quote-blindness gap on the same artifact and line -- that fix's harness tested only single-line quoted mentions. Distinct from the target-substrate scope-blindness gap on the hook family.

evidence.reference: perl -pe strips per line; multi-line quoted spans never collapse
recognizer matches the surviving CLI-name mention in an un-stripped multi-line quoted body
the prior fix's recognizer false-positive row is single-line only -- no multi-line row exercises the line-scoping
investigation of record: skeleton dump, control boundary matrix, slurp-mode fix verified 28/28 plus controls plus true positives on a copy, fail-closed degradation argument

impact: Any Bash command that both mentions pi-context inside a multi-line quoted argument (a forensic commit message describing pi-context work; a heredoc or JSON payload discussing the CLI) and contains a shell metacharacter elsewhere is blocked though it invokes no op -- observed blocking legitimate mid-merge build+commit work twice this session. The multi-line dimension is where forensic commit messages about this very tooling live, so the guard most often misfires on commits describing pi-context changes.
proposed_resolution: Make the strip whole-command: perl -0777 -pe (slurp) so the substitution runs once over the full multi-line command and quoted spans collapse across newlines exactly as single-line spans do -- verified on a copy: both live-bite cases pass, a multi-line single-quoted payload with interior metacharacters passes, both true positives still block, the existing harness is 28/28, and unbalanced quoting degrades fail-closed. Segment-scoping the glue checks is rejected: the branches are already recognizer-gated, and segment-scoping would let a real op with ancillary glue in another segment through. Extend the harness with multi-line rows (quoted commit-message mention passes; multi-line quoted payload with data metacharacters passes; a genuine multi-line pipe true-positive blocks). Copy-first guard development. Completes the multi-line facet of the closed quote-blindness gap.
